description: Popular poetry in Judeao-Arabic. Vocalized. Rudimentary hand. Includes a verse beginning "go up to Jūjar" (אטלע לגוגר יא אנסאן). In the same style as Nāṣir and includes his typical sign off "anā al-adīb al-ʿibrī" (see Elbaum, Fragment of the Month, October 2021). (He then continues, "and I frittered away my life in sinning" (wa-fī l-dhunūb ḍāʿ ʿumrī)). However, the handwriting seems different than that of all the other Nāṣir fragments. Needs further examination.
description_he: טקסט ספרותי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
